title: AEP v1.2 F24 Evidence Rights & Redaction Record
description: v1.2 immune-system primitive. Every evidence item gets a visibility class so AEP is safe for normal people, schools, companies, sensitive workflows (operator source.md L29 + L186-192). 7 visibility classes verbatim from L188-190: public / private / local_only / hashed_only / encrypted / ephemeral / forbidden_to_export. Lineage EXTENDS GDPR Article 25 privacy-by-design + capability security + differential privacy fundamentals per operator L33 + plan sec7. HV5 closure: salted_hash REQUIRES per_packet_random_salt:true (NOT corpus-shared); hash_correlation_resistance documented; frequency-analysis attack noted as known limitation; sensitive workflows MUST use encrypted or forbidden_to_export tier. Composes_with: v1.1 F18 SourceProvenanceGraph.

bound_to_evidence_id.description: Evidence row id (source, span, claim, review, etc.) this classification applies to.

evidence_kind.description: Which canonical 7-file row class this evidence belongs to.

visibility_class.description: Operator L188-190 verbatim 7-class enum. HARD-CONSTRAINED per HV5 closure: schema enforces all 7 classes; no string-label override permitted.

salt_scope.description: HV5 closure HARD-CONSTRAINED. Adversary A5 attack: corpus-shared salt leaks via frequency analysis on cross-packet hashes. Required value: per_packet_random_salt. per_corpus_shared_salt EXPLICITLY documented as INSUFFICIENT for sensitive workflows. no_salt REJECTED on hashed_only class.

salt_present.description: Whether a salt is actually present in the redacted artifact. If visibility_class == hashed_only && salt_present == false, validator MUST emit AEP12_F24_HASH_UNSALTED warning.

salt_storage_class.description: Where the salt itself lives. Salt MUST NOT be public or hashed_only (recursion).

frequency_analysis_attack_acknowledged.description: HV5 closure: for hashed_only tier, frequency-analysis attack is a known limitation. Schema requires explicit acknowledgement. Sensitive workflows MUST use encrypted or forbidden_to_export instead.

frequency_analysis_attack_text.description: When acknowledged==true, the exact text disclosed in export manifest. Civilian-readable. Adversary A5 cheapest disconfirmer test fixture path.

disclosed_in_export.description: Operator L29: 'export manifests that clearly say what was removed.' If visibility_class != public, MUST be true.

what_was_removed.description: Plain-language description of redacted content. Examples: 'private user file paths', 'reviewer identity', 'salted source hashes', 'all encrypted artifact contents'.

what_was_kept.description: Plain-language description of what survives export. Example: 'claim text, falsifier outcome, count of sources'.

redaction_method.description: How the redaction was applied. value_replaced_with_hash MUST use per_packet_random_salt per HV5.

external_precedents.description: F18 lineage. F24 EXTENDS: GDPR Article 25 privacy-by-design + capability security + differential privacy fundamentals.

hash_correlation_resistance.frequency_analysis_attack_text: Cross-packet hash correlation could in theory reveal that the same file was used across packets. Per-packet random salt makes this attack require corpus-wide salt recovery, which is computationally infeasible at the per-packet entropy this schema enforces.

export_manifest_disclosure.what_was_removed: Your uploaded lease PDF file contents and original file path.
export_manifest_disclosure.what_was_kept: A salted hash that proves the lease summary referred to a real file, without revealing its contents or location.

lineage_basis.verifying_grep: rg 'gdpr|privacy by design|differential privacy|capability security' --type md research/sources/
